_note: Shipped DEFAULT phrase+notice pair for hooks/demurral_detect.py's static tier (mechanisms.demurral_detect mode="static", or mode="observe"'s fallback when the classifier is unavailable). Source: ADR-0013 Rule 3's own canonical demurral vocabulary (law/adr/0013-execution-integrity.md, Rule 3 body) plus that same hook's own docstring enumeration (which additionally names "YAGNI" and "gold-plating"); "overkill" is a maintainer-added item (2026-07-17) not verbatim in Rule 3's text but matching the eval corpus's own POSITIVE specimens (instruments/demurral_corpus.jsonl rows 7 and 62). The phrase list is a DELIBERATELY WEAK, enumeration-fails-open (ADR-0011 Rule 4) list -- a static, case-insensitive, word-boundary match, not a claim of the classifier's shape-based coverage.
_override: A deployment may replace this file WHOLESALE (not merged) at <world-root>/.claude/demurral_phrases.json -- same shape as this file: either a bare JSON array of phrase strings (phrases only, built-in notice), or an object (this shape) with a 'phrases' array of non-empty strings and an optional 'notice' string. Start by copying this file there and editing freely; the override, once present and valid, IS the effective phrase list AND notice in full. A present-but-malformed override degrades loudly (one stderr warning) back to this shipped default, never to a silently empty list or a crash. See hooks/demurral_detect.py's module docstring (STATIC TIER / NOTICE CONFIGURATION sections) for the full mechanism, and bootstrap/templates/APPARATUS.md's demurral_detect note for the operator-facing summary.
_notice_note: 'notice' is the text injected into the agent's context on a static-tier hit (hooks/demurral_detect.py::_emit_static_notice). It carries this file's own current built-in text verbatim, so a deployment copying this file to author its own vocabulary sees both knobs -- phrases and notice -- in one place. It may contain the placeholder '{phrases}', substituted (via str.replace, never str.format, so stray braces in operator text never raise) with the comma-joined, repr-quoted phrase(s) this invocation matched. 'notice' is optional: a missing/absent key, or an explicit null, falls back to the hardcoded built-in text (hooks/demurral_detect.py's DEFAULT_NOTICE); a present-but-non-string value warns loudly on stderr (naming the offending value) and also falls back to the built-in text -- never a crash, never a silently empty notice. This field is held byte-identical to hooks/demurral_detect.py's DEFAULT_NOTICE by seen-red/demurral-detector/red-specimen.py's drift check (test/CI gate, ADR-0011 Rule 1 vocabulary) -- case 10 in that suite reads this field directly and compares it against the hook's own constant, failing loudly (printing both) on any drift. NOT held in sync by discipline/comment alone, and never was verified to be until that case existed.
notice: content below contains the phrase {phrases}, which is in ADR-0013 Rule 3's canonical demurral vocabulary. Be mindful of Rule 3 and reconsider whether this narrows already-mandated work. This is a free, enumeration-only word match with no judgment of context or shape -- it is disregardable if this is a neutral scope question to the ratifier, a fair-dealing renegotiation of a spec found wrong (with evidence), or a genuine external bound reported upward; you, the agent, judge whether this is a false positive.
